title: Online Estimation of Partial Transpose Moments via Fast Classical Updates
abstract: Partial-transpose (PT) moments are among the most practically relevant nonlinear quantities accessible from local Pauli classical shadows, because they directly underpin mixed-state entanglement certification and recent PT-moment-based phase diagnostics. The online framework of Marso \emph{et al.} rewrote the exact PT-moment statistic into a fixed-memory recurrence that updates a small collection of accumulated matrices after each new shadow snapshot. Its update cost is independent of the shot number, but each step treats the incoming partially transposed snapshot as a generic dense matrix. Therefore, the arithmetic cost scales cubically with the dimension of the Hilbert space. We show that the same estimator can be updated exactly in subcubic time per shot while retaining the same memory. The key point is that the accumulated matrices become dense, but the fresh partially transposed snapshot still factorizes into local factors. Right-multiplication by that factorized snapshot can therefore be executed by exact column-pair sweeps. For the second PT moment, we further optimize the process by utilizing a Pauli basis update.
summary: This paper introduces a more efficient way to calculate entanglement-related quantities (PT moments) using classical shadows. By exploiting the local structure of quantum measurements, the authors reduce the computational complexity from cubic to subcubic scaling, enabling the analysis of larger quantum systems.
main_problem: The computational cost of updating partial-transpose (PT) moments in the classical shadows framework scales cubically with the Hilbert space dimension, making it inefficient for large systems.
main_result: The authors present a subcubic-time update algorithm for PT-moment estimators that maintains the same memory requirements as previous methods by exploiting the factorized structure of incoming snapshots.
method: The method utilizes an online framework where accumulated matrices are updated via exact column-pair sweeps, leveraging the fact that fresh partially transposed snapshots remain factorized into local factors.
model_or_system: The paper focuses on the framework of local Pauli classical shadows used for estimating nonlinear properties of quantum states.
key_observables: Partial-transpose (PT) moments, used for entanglement certification and phase diagnostics.
main_assumptions: The algorithm assumes the use of local Pauli classical shadows and that the incoming snapshots can be treated as factorized local operators.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of cubic scaling in PT-moment updates, proposes a subcubic solution using column-pair sweeps on factorized snapshots, and provides an optimized Pauli basis update for the second PT moment.
